key_points: More from TechCrunch AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round AI inference company Baseten is close to finalizing a stunning $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion.

Launched in 2019, Baseten is a startup benefiting from what The Next Wave hailed the “ inference gold rush ,” in which VCs are pouring enormous amounts of money into companies building the inference layer.
Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.
Just five months ago, the startup announced that it had raised a $300 million Series E at a $5 billion valuation.
And that round was just nine months after raising a $150 million Series D.

The 11 standout startups from YC’s Demo Day, according to VCs General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming - now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI AI.
Inference is what the model does after a user submits a prompt.
Some investors in this latest funding round are reportedly coming in at a $13 billion valuation, while others at $11 billion, sources told the Journal.
Baseten promises to handle inference quickly while controlling costs by routing requests to the best-for-task model, especially to competent, less-expensive open source alternatives.
If finalized, this latest round would represent a 160% increase in valuation in less than half a year.
